content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, the integer KKK is said to be AAA-good if the following condition holds:


gcd⁡(Ai,K)=gcd⁡(Aj,K)\gcd(A_i, K) = \gcd(A_j, K)gcd(Ai​,K)=gcd(Aj​,K) for every 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N.
Define f(A)f(A)f(A) to be the number of integers between 111 and MMM that are AAA-good.

You are given integers NNN and MMM. Compute the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all MNM^NMN arrays AAA of length NNN whose elements lie between 111 and MMM.

This sum can be large, so compute it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the length of the array and the maximum allowed element.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all MNM^NMN arrays, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.
